html, body { overflow-x:hidden!important; } body { position: relative!important;} ul, ol{ margin-left: 0!important; } .stk-block.stk-block-column.stk-column>.stk-column-wrapper{ margin-left:0; } p, time, span, ul, ol, .gform-body, .tribe-events-widget-events-list__events, .tribe-events-widget-events-list__event-title, .tribe-event-date-start, .tribe-events-widget-events-list__event-date-tag-daynum{ font-family:'Montserrat'!important; } main p, main li{ color: black; } a{ font-weight: 500; } main{ min-height: 550px; } .teen-icon i{ font-size: 24px; } /* HEADER */ .builder-item--header_contact ul{ list-style:none!important; } .c-type-text span, .builder-header-html_2-item p{ color: #1a2b5c; font-size: 16px!important; } .c-type-text span i{ font-size: 22px!important; margin-right: 5px; } #header_contact{ margin-bottom: 0px!important; } .mega-row .widgettitle{ font-size: 16px!important; font-weight: bold!important;} /* MENU */ @media screen and (min-width: 1024px) { .mega-row{ background-color: white!Important; display:flex!important; padding: 1.2em!important; } .mega-row .widgettitle{ font-size: 20px!important; font-weight: bold!important; color: #1a2b5c!important; } .mega-col{ padding: 2%!important; max-width: 50%!important; } .mega-items .menu-item a{ color:black!important; text-transform: capitalize!important; font-size: 16px!important; font-weight: normal!important; } .widget_nav_menu ul{ display:flex!important; flex-direction: column!important; } .widget_nav_menu ul li a{ padding: 10px 0px!important; } .mega-items .menu-item a:hover{ text-decoration: underline!important; } } /* PAGE COVERS */ #page-cover:before{ background: linear-gradient(90deg,rgba(0, 0, 0, 0.65) 60%, rgba(0, 0, 0, 0) 100%); } .page-cover-title{ font-weight: 600; } .page-cover-tagline{ font-size: 20px; margin-bottom: 0px } /* HOME PAGE */ #wp-block-search__input-1, .wp-block-search__button{ font-family: 'Montserrat'; } .wp-block-search__button{ text-transform: capitalize!important; font-weight: 500!important; border: 2px solid #1a2b5c!important; } .wp-block-search__button:hover{ background-color: white!important; color: #1a2b5c!important; } .wp-block-search__input, .wp-block-search__button{ border-radius: 30px!important; } /* SIDEBARS */ #sidebar-primary{ background-color: #f0f4f6!important; } #sidebar-primary h4{ font-size: 20px; font-weight: 600; } .widget_text{ margin-bottom: 5px!important; } #sidebar-primary .widget_nav_menu li a{ border-bottom: 0px!important; padding: 2px!important; } /* RESOURCES */ .searchandfilter ul{ display: flex; flex-wrap: wrap; align-items: center; } .searchandfilter ul li { margin-right: 15px; } .search-filter-results h3{ font-size: 20px; } input[type="submit"], button{ background-color: #1a2b5c!important; border-radius: 30px!important; font-family:'Montserrat'; text-transform: capitalize!important; font-weight: 500!important; border: solid 2px #1a2b5c!important; } button a{ color: white!important; } button a:hover{ color: #1a2b5c!important; } input[type="submit"]:hover, button:hover{ background-color: white!important; color: #1a2b5c!important; } .sf-input-select, .sf-input-text{ font-family: 'Montserrat'!important; color: black!important; } .sf-input-text{ box-shadow: none!important; } /* E-RESOURCES Widgets */ /* FOOTER */ .footer-copyright{ text-align:center!important; } #block-18 h2, #block-9 h2, #block-10 h2, #block-20 h2{ text-transform: capitalize!important; font-size: 20px; font-weight: 800; } #block-18, #block-9, #block-10, #block-20{ margin-bottom: 5px; } #block-9, #block-10, #block-20{ border-bottom: 1px solid white; padding-bottom: 5px; } .footer-main-inner a{ text-decoration: underline!important; } #site-footer .widget li a{ border-bottom: 0px!important; padding: 0px!important; color: white!important; text-decoration: none; } Skip to content Menu Close Official Website of the Billerica Public Library

pass_page.policy_text:
To limit museum types being displayed, check the corresponding categories:
To select a museum, click “Details/Reserve”, then select which Pass to reserve.
{"@context": "http://schema.org","@type": "CivicStructure","address": "568 Main Street, Hudson, MA 01749","logo": "https://s3.amazonaws.com/ai-assets/museum-logos/ma-american-heritage-museum_150_md.jpg","telephone": "978-562-9182","name": "American Heritage Museum","url": "","description": "The American Heritage Museum, located in Hudson, MA, is dedicated to military history. Exhibits examine conflicts from the Revolutionary War through 9/11 and the &ldquo;War on Terror.&rdquo; A good portion of the museum is focused on the sizable Littlefield collection, which includes many rare items from the U.S. and other countries. Among other artifacts, visitors to the museum will encounter aircraft, tanks, guns, and automobiles, some dating from the beginning of the 20th century."} American Heritage Museum 568 Main Street, Hudson, MA 01749978-562-9182https://www.americanheritagemuseum.org/[Details/Reserve]
American Heritage Museum The American Heritage Museum, located in Hudson, MA, is dedicated to military history. Exhibits examine conflicts from the Revolutionary War through 9/11 and the “War on Terror.” A good portion of the museum is focused on the sizable Littlefield collection, which includes many rare items from the U.S. and other countries. Among other artifacts, visitors to the museum will encounter aircraft, tanks, guns, and automobiles, some dating from the beginning of the 20th century.
Categories: Family, History, Military
Pass Type: Circulating Pass (must be picked up and returned to the branch)
Pass Benefits Pass admits up to six visitors at half-price admission. Regular admission is $23.00 for adults, $20.00 for seniors, veterans, and active duty service members with ID, and $12.00 for children under 16. Children under 3 years old are admitted for free. World War II and Korean War Veterans are admitted for free.  Passes are not valid for special events. 
This physical pass may be picked up at the circulation desk the day before use. Please return this pass to the Billerica Public Library by 9am the next day. The pass may be returned to the outside drop box during off hours.
This pass is generously sponsored by the Family Friends of the Billerica Public Library.
Reserve a Pass Reserve a Pass
{"@context": "http://schema.org","@type": "CivicStructure","address": "308 Congress Street, Boston, MA 02210","logo": "https://s3.amazonaws.com/ai-assets/museum-logos/ma-boston-childrens-museum_3_md.jpg","telephone": "617-426-6500","name": "Boston Children&apos;s Museum","url": "","description": "Founded in 1913 by the Science Teachers&apos; Bureau, the Boston Children&apos;s Museum is the second oldest children&apos;s museum in the world and one of the few that maintain a collection. Designed for both children and families, the Museum exhibits focus on science, culture, environmental awareness, health & fitness, and the arts, emphasizing hands-on engagement and learning through experience, and employing play as a tool to spark the inherent creativity, curiosity, and imagination of children."} Boston Children's Museum 308 Congress Street, Boston, MA 02210617-426-6500http://www.bostonchildrensmuseum.org/[Details/Reserve]
Boston Children's Museum Founded in 1913 by the Science Teachers' Bureau, the Boston Children's Museum is the second oldest children's museum in the world and one of the few that maintain a collection. Designed for both children and families, the Museum exhibits focus on science, culture, environmental awareness, health & fitness, and the arts, emphasizing hands-on engagement and learning through experience, and employing play as a tool to spark the inherent creativity, curiosity, and imagination of children.
Categories: Children, Crafts, Family, Technology
Pass Type: Printable/Digital Coupon Pass (link delivered by email)
Pass Benefits Digital pass admits up to 4 visitors at half-price admission. General admission rates are $24.00 per person. Infants under one year old are free.
Online timed-tickets must be purchased in advance at the Children’s Museum website. Be sure to reserve “Half Price Library Timed Tickets” under the “Discount Programs” section at www.bostonchildrensmuseum.org/ticketing. Upon arrival at the museum, you will have to show your tickets and library pass for admission. The date on the pass must match the date of your visit.
You may print the attached pass at home, display it on your smartphone, or pick up a printed copy at the library.
*Please note that the Boston Children's Museum's hours are subject to change. Please check the official website for all updates: bostonchildrensmuseum.org.
{"@context": "http://schema.org","@type": "CivicStructure","address": "15 State Street, Suite 1100, Boston, MA 02109","logo": "https://s3.amazonaws.com/ai-assets/museum-logos/ma-boston-harbor-islands_6_md.jpg","telephone": "617-223-8666","name": "Boston Harbor Island Ferry","url": "","description": "The largest recreational open space in Eastern Massachusetts, the Boston Harbor Islands National and State Park is comprised of 34 islands and mainland parks that deliver access to historic sites and provide opportunities to hike and camp. Transportation to the islands is by ferry and, depending on the location, your own boat. Please refer to their website for the most up-to-date information about what is open and how to get there."} Boston Harbor Island Ferry 15 State Street, Suite 1100, Boston, MA 02109617-223-8666http://www.bostonharborislands.org/[Details/Reserve]
Boston Harbor Island Ferry The largest recreational open space in Eastern Massachusetts, the Boston Harbor Islands National and State Park is comprised of 34 islands and mainland parks that deliver access to historic sites and provide opportunities to hike and camp. Transportation to the islands is by ferry and, depending on the location, your own boat. Please refer to their website for the most up-to-date information about what is open and how to get there.
Categories: Family, Nature, Recreation
Pass Benefits This pass provides a 50% off discount on Boston Harbor Island Ferry tickets for up to 4 people.
This pass is a digital discount code. You will receive the code via email after reserving this pass. You will submit the discount code when purchasing tickets online at www.bostonharborislands.org.
The discount code can be used throughout the entire 2026 season, which is listed below:
May 15 - June 14: Thursdays through Sundays
June 16 - September 1: every day
July 15 - September 1: every day
September 4 - 21: Thursdays through Sundays
September 26 - October 13: Fridays through Sundays
Peddocks Island from Hingham
